verdict_reasoning: BAX is a mitochondrial outer membrane protein of the BCL2 family, functioning as a pro-apoptotic regulator. Its mechanism of action involves interaction with mitochondrial VDAC and regulation of mitochondrial membrane permeability. It does not localize to or function at the plasma membrane outer face. No surface-directed therapeutics target BAX, and there is no evidence of plasma membrane trafficking, TM anchoring, or surface accessibility.
